content.full_text:
OFFICE OF COUNTY COUNSEL
COUNTY OF BUTTE
25 COUNTY CENTER DRIVE / OROVILLE, CALIFORNIA 95965-3819 (916) 538-7621 / FAX (916) 538-7120

FEB 1 1 1991

NEIL H. McCABE
CHIEF DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL

SUSAN ROFF MINASIAN
COUNTY COUNSEL

DAVID M. McCLAIN
CHIEF DEPUTY COUNTY COUNSEL

February 11, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814
Attention: Legal Division

Re: Request for Informal Assistance

Dear Legal Division Staff:

The County of Butte is in the process of updating its
Conflict of Interest Code. In the proposed new code three
positions within the District Attorney's office have been
designated as positions for which statements of economic interest
shall be filed. The proposed code places these positions in the
broadest disclosure category requiring the disclosure of all
sources of income, interests in real property and investments and
business positions in business entities located in or doing
business in Butte County. The three positions are: Chief Deputy
District Attorney-Civil; Chief Deputy District Attorney-Criminal;
and Chief Investigator/Administrative Assistant.

This proposal is a departure from the present County
Conflict of Interest Code which did not require any person in the
District Attorney's Office to file disclosure statements. (Of
course, the District Attorney himself has been required to file
pursuant to state law.)

The District Attorney, the two Chief Deputy District
Attorneys and the Chief Investigator/Administrative Assistant
have all expressed concerns regarding the proposed designations.
Their concerns have been expressed both in writing (see enclosed
copy of memorandum to Board of Supervisors dated December 18,
1990) and orally. Among the concerns expressed are that the
positions in question do not involve the making or participation
in the making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material
effect on any financial interest. Elaborating on their position,
they have pointed out that any decision regarding the expenditure
of three hundred or more dollars for goods and services are made
only by the District Attorney himself and are not delegated to
any other staff member. The Chief Deputies have also pointed out
the remoteness between their official duties and any personal
financial interest, have pointed out that independent ethical
considerations would require them to disqualify themselves or be

-1-

recused from any case involving any personal interest and have
expressed concern that the disclosure by them of information
about themselves could make them vulnerable to members of the
criminal population with which they deal.

I am enclosing for your review job descriptions for the
three positions in question. I am also enclosing job
descriptions for the Deputy District Attorney's, since the Chief
Deputies have asked why their positions have been singled out to
file disclosure statements when in many respects their duties
are similar to those of the Deputy District Attorneys. Neither
the current nor the proposed County Code require the Deputy
District Attorneys to file disclosure statements.

We would appreciate receiving an opinion from you whether or
not    the Chief    Deputy    positions    or    the Chief
Investigator/Administrator are of a type which (within the
meaning of Government Code 87300) involve the making or
participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably
have a material effect on any financial interest, and, if so,
which types of investments, interests in real property and
sources of income should be reportable by them.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. We would
appreciate hearing from you as soon as possible since the
rProposed Conflict of Interest Code is scheduled for a continued
hearing before the Board of Supervisors on April 16, 1991.

Very truly yours,

SUSAN MINASIAN
Butte County Counsel

By___________________
Neil H. McCabe
Assistant County Counsel

NHM/slt
(fppc1.ltr)

cc: Mike Ramsey, District Attorney
    Howard Abbott, Chief Deputy District Attorney-Civil
    Helen Harberts, Chief Deputy District Attorney-Criminal
    Bob Peterson, Chief Investigator/Administrative Assistant
    Carol Roach, Assistant Clerk of the Board of Supervisors

Enclosure


-2-
